pattern: Binary search the answer length and test each candidate length with a rolling hash, since a duplicate of length L guarantees one of every shorter length exists.
hints: If some duplicated substring of length L exists, a duplicated substring of every length below L exists too — that monotonicity invites binary search on the length.
For a fixed length, you need to detect whether any window repeats; hashing every window lets you check that in near-linear time.
Binary search L; for each L roll a polynomial hash over all length-L windows and report a repeat, keeping the actual matching substring when one is found.

edge_inputs.description: whole string is a doubled block

stress.description: moderate strings over a tiny alphabet so long duplicates appear
stress.note: three-letter alphabet forces frequent long repeats; size kept modest for the quadratic oracle
